package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository type is defined outside the repository package.
// Here, AccountRepository is defined in the handler package instead of a
// dedicated repository/repo/store package.

type Account struct {
	ID      string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Name    string `gorm:"column:name"`
	Balance int    `gorm:"column:balance"`
}

// AccountRepository is defined in the handler package - anti-pattern.
// Repository types should live in a repository, repo, or store package.
type AccountRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

func (r *AccountRepository) FindByID(id string) (*Account, error) {
	var account Account
	err := r.db.First(&account, "id = ?", id).Error
	return &account, err
}

func (r *AccountRepository) Create(account *Account) error {
	return r.db.Create(account).Error
}

// AccountHandler uses the repository defined in the same (handler) package.
type AccountHandler struct {
	repo *AccountRepository
}

func NewAccountHandler(db *gorm.DB) *AccountHandler {
	return &AccountHandler{
		repo: &AccountRepository{db: db},
	}
}

func (h *AccountHandler) GetAccount(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")
	account, err := h.repo.FindByID(id)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(404, gin.H{"error": "not found"})
		return
	}
	c.JSON(200, account)
}
